ccel.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

title: To Martin, Scholasticus.

source.title: NPNF-213. Gregory the Great (II), Ephraim Syrus, Aphrahat

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Seeing that questions arising in civil affairs need, as is known to thy Greatness, very full enquiry, let thy wisdom consider with what care and vigilance the causes of bishops should be investigated.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, in the letter which thou hast sent us by the bearer of these presents on the questions with respect to which thou wert sent to us by our brother and fellow-bishop Crementius, thou hast given only a superficial account of them, and hast been entirely silent about their root.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But, had their origin and intrinsic character been manifest to us, we should have known what should be decided about them, and would then settle the mind of our aforesaid brother by a plain and suitable reply.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: This, however, is altogether displeasing to us, that thou givest us to understand that some of the bishops have gone to the court without letters from their primate, and that they hold unlawful assemblies.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: But since, as we have before said, the origin and nature of the questions are entirely unknown to us, we cannot pronounce anything definitely, lest, as would be very reprehensible, we should seem to pass sentence about things imperfectly known.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Hence it was very needful that, for our complete information, thy Greatness should have proceeded hither to reply to our questions during the time of thy lingering in Sicily.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: Nevertheless, now that thou hast seen our brother and fellow-bishop John, we believe that in him thou hast seen us also.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And so since he has been at pains himself also to write to us about the same questions, we have written in reply to him what seemed to us right.

verses.book: Selected Epistles of Gregory the Great.
verses.text: And, since he is a priest of ripe and cautious judgment, if you are willing to treat with him on the questions which he has been commissioned to entertain, we are sure that you will find in him what is both advantageous and reasonable.
